section.heading: Environmental and climate justice block grants

subsection.heading: Appropriation
subsection.chapeau: In addition to amounts otherwise available, there is appropriated to the Administrator for fiscal year 2022, out of any money in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated—

paragraph.content: $2,800,000,000 to remain available until September 30, 2026, to award grants for the activities described in subsection (b); and
$200,000,000 to remain available until September 30, 2026, to provide technical assistance to eligible entities related to grants awarded under this section.

content.p: The Administrator shall use amounts made available under subsection (a)(1) to award grants for periods of up to 3 years to eligible entities to carry out activities described in paragraph (2) that benefit disadvantaged communities, as defined by the Administrator.

paragraph.heading: Eligible activities
paragraph.chapeau: An eligible entity may use a grant awarded under this subsection for—

subparagraph.content: community-led air and other pollution monitoring, prevention, and remediation, and investments in low- and zero-emission and resilient technologies and related infrastructure and workforce development that help reduce greenhouse gas emissions and other air pollutants;
mitigating climate and health risks from urban heat islands, extreme heat, wood heater emissions, and wildfire events;
reducing indoor toxics and indoor air pollution; or
facilitating engagement of disadvantaged communities in State and Federal advisory groups, workshops, rulemakings, and other public processes.

paragraph.heading: Eligible entities
paragraph.chapeau: In this subsection, the term “eligible entity” means—

clause.content: an Indian tribe, a local government, or an institution of higher education; and

subparagraph.content: a partnership of community-based nonprofit organizations.

content.p: The Administrator shall reserve 7 percent of the amounts made available under subsection (a) for administrative costs to carry out this section.
In this section, the term “greenhouse gas” means the air pollutants carbon dioxide, hydrofluorocarbons, methane, nitrous oxide, perfluorocarbons, and sulfur hexafluoride.
